subject_profile.summary: Base country-level assessment; default category weights prioritize rule of law, institutional integrity, and personal freedom.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

flags: Generated 2026-05-28 under v2.0: rebuilt from independent sources (UN/World Bank/IMF estimates, Freedom House, RSF, HRW, Amnesty, OHCHR, IPC, OCHA, WHO); neither warring party (SAF nor RSF) used as a governance/data source.

An active catastrophic civil war since April 2023 with no functioning government, documented atrocities/ethnic cleansing, and the world's largest displacement crisis. Data is fragmentary; figures are external estimates.
Standout drags: a collapsed war economy with hyperinflation, mass atrocities and famine, and total institutional collapse. Among the most negative profiles in the dataset.

economic.notes: A collapsed war economy with hyperinflation and a non-functional central bank.

fiscal_state.notes: GDP contracted catastrophically (a roughly 30% fall in 2023), with revenue collapsed and extreme poverty soaring. (IMF/World Bank estimates)

monetary_independence.notes: No functional central bank; the parallel market sets the rate. (Sudan Tribune; IMF)

inflation.notes: Hyperinflation, with purchasing power down 70-90%. (IMF estimates)

banking_stability.notes: The formal banking system fractured into a shadow economy with no reserves. (independent reporting)

trade_actions_capacity.notes: Gold smuggled to fund the war, agriculture destroyed, and famine declared. (Chatham House; IPC)

institutional.notes: No functional state, collapsed data, and among the worst press repression.

rule_of_law.notes: No functional state - a war between two military factions, with a UN fact-finding mission finding genocidal intent. (OHCHR; HRW)

statistical_integrity.notes: Collapsed and largely absent - external estimates only. (World Bank caveats)

civil_service_capacity.notes: State administrative capacity collapsed, with de-facto fragmentation. (BTI 2026)

press_freedom.notes: A near-bottom RSF standing, with journalists killed, detained, and convoys struck. (RSF)

political_social.notes: An active catastrophic civil war with mass atrocities and the world's largest displacement crisis.

political_stability.notes: An active catastrophic civil war since April 2023 with no functioning government and territorial fragmentation. (Freedom House)

civil_liberties.notes: Mass atrocities, ethnic massacres (Darfur/El Fasher), and systematic sexual violence. (Amnesty; OHCHR)

treatment_non_citizens.notes: A war state with ethnically-targeted killings - not a host environment. (HRW)

social_cohesion.notes: Shattered along ethnic and regional lines by the war and famine. (Atlantic Council)

demographics.notes: Around 12M+ displaced (the world's largest crisis), with famine deaths and catastrophic child malnutrition. (OCHA; UN)

geopolitical.notes: A fragmented proxy battleground and the world's worst active humanitarian crisis.

alliance_reliability.notes: A fragmented battleground - the RSF UAE-backed, the SAF Egypt/Saudi-backed - a proxy war. (Atlantic Council)

conflict_involvement.notes: The world's worst active war and humanitarian crisis. (WHO; OCHA)

sanctions_capital_controls.notes: Sanctioned commanders and arms-embargo violations - isolated. (Chatham House)

physical_practical.notes: A collapsed health system amid famine and cholera, with war-destroyed infrastructure.

climate.notes: ND-GAIN bottom band - Sahel drought, extreme heat, Nile/flash flooding, war-collapsed readiness. (ND-GAIN)

healthcare.notes: Over 70% of hospitals destroyed, an IPC-5 famine, and cholera - among the world's worst. (WHO)

infrastructure.notes: War-destroyed, especially in Darfur and Kordofan. (WHO; Soufan Center)

crime_safety.notes: War and atrocity zones, kidnap-for-ransom, and mass executions. (HRW; Amnesty)

summary: A country consumed by an active, catastrophic civil war since April 2023 between two military factions, with no functioning government, documented atrocities and ethnic massacres (a UN mission has found genocidal intent), and the world's largest displacement crisis (12M+). The economy has collapsed into hyperinflation and a shadow banking system, gold is smuggled to fund the war, agriculture is destroyed, and an IPC-5 famine and cholera ravage a health system with most hospitals out of service. Among the most negative profiles in the dataset; figures are external estimates.
